violations.claim: On fileIssue exception, errors++ and the issue is silently dropped.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 161
; On fileIssue exception, errors++ and the issue is silently dropped.
; There is no retry queue, no persistence, no dead-letter mechanism.
(declare-const errors_final Int)
(declare-const issues_permanently_lost Int)
; Transition: each caught exception increments errors and discards the issue
(assert (>= errors_final 1))
(assert (= issues_permanently_lost errors_final))  ; 1:1 correspondence
; Violation: neurallog violations are permanently lost on transient failure
(assert (> issues_permanently_lost 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → errors=2: two violations identified by neurallog are silently gone

violations.claim: If gh CLI is unavailable, every fileIssue call throws and is caught.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 161
; If gh CLI is unavailable, every fileIssue call throws and is caught.
; The function returns {filed:0, skipped:0, errors:N} without raising an exception.
; The caller must explicitly inspect errors > 0 to detect total failure.
(declare-const num_issues Int)
(declare-const filed Int)
(declare-const skipped Int)
(declare-const errors Int)
; Transition: gh CLI unavailable — every fileIssue call throws, caught by try/catch
(assert (>= num_issues 1))
(assert (= errors num_issues))    ; every issue lands in the catch branch
(assert (= filed 0))
(assert (= skipped 0))
; Conservation still holds even in total failure
(assert (= (+ filed (+ skipped errors)) num_issues))
; Violation: all issues lost, function exits normally — no exception propagates to caller
(assert (= filed 0))
(assert (> errors 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → gh not installed: N issues lost, caller receives {filed:0, skipped:0, errors:N}
